title: N/S Only
subject: 82-A: N/S Axis
body: Only North and South are open; East and West are strapped out for this burn. SEC-082 audit staff want a clean hourly discharge proof on the live axis only.
bullets: [GOAL] Clear 60 vehicles; at least 15 per direction on N/S.
[RESTRICTION] E/W closed. Max 2 phases
[HAZARD] Gridlock on any approach halts the docket.
bureauMemo: Mayoral district wants East Street looking spotless on paper during the audit window. Budget Office loves opening Form B-19; do not give them an excuse.

quickRef.body: Just make North and South go at the same time.

editorQuickRef.body:
1) Rack: tap + to add a phase.
2) Builder: NORTH → ALL → .GO.
3) Rack: tap + again for phase 2.
4) Builder: SOUTH → ALL → .GO.
5) Scroll down, [ VALIDATION ] then [ RUN ] when the rack is clean.

title: E/W Only
subject: 82-B: E/W Axis
body: North and South are locked for diagnostic burn-in; traffic is only entering from East and West. Clear the spike without cooking the relay shelf past the thermal duty envelope.
bullets: [GOAL] Clear 75 vehicles; at least 20 per direction on E/W.
[RESTRICTION] N/S closed. Max 2 phases
[HAZARD] Saturated buffers (queues backing up hard) trigger gridlock halt.
bureauMemo: Treasury pre-capped this corridor: extra phase banks book as capital, not O&M. Legal will not backfill an unapproved shelf.

quickRef.body: Same idea as N/S, but East and West go together. Two phases, no tricks.

editorQuickRef.body: Same rack + builder flow as 1A—only East and West are live here. Burn when it compiles clean.

title: Full Compass
subject: 82-C: All Approaches
body: Every approach is live and registers are deep. Run four banks that cover the full compass while staying inside the sync envelope Finance signed.
bullets: [GOAL] Clear 85 vehicles; at least 22 per direction.
[RESTRICTION] Max 4 phases; Hardware cost ceiling 1900.
[HAZARD] Gridlock or incompatible greens in one phase fail the run.
bureauMemo: Commissioner's nephew lives on the North approach. Try not to keep him waiting, or Facilities will hear about it.

quickRef.body: All four approaches are live. Four phases max—cover the compass without illegal overlaps.

title: Protected Lefts
subject: 82-D: Left vs Through
body: Northbound and southbound left turns cannot share a phase with opposing through traffic. Give N_LEFT and S_LEFT their own protected banks.
bullets: [GOAL] Clear 70 vehicles; at least 12 per direction on N/S.
[RESTRICTION] E/W closed. Max 4 phases
[HAZARD] Turn-through overlap in the same phase voids the run (conflict fault).
initialCode:
phase(1):
    NORTH_STRAIGHT.GO
    SOUTH_STRAIGHT.GO

phase(2):
    # Add Left Turn Logic here
bureauMemo: OGAS counsel still bills by the footnote. Discovery on the last carve-out ran three fiscal years; try not to star in the sequel.

quickRef.body: N/S left turns can't share a phase with E/W straight. Give lefts their own protected phase.

title: Heavy North
subject: 83-A: Asymmetric Load
body: Spawn ROM is skewed: northbound demand dominates the morning peak. Meet quota on the heavy mast without starving southbound below audit minimums.
bullets: [GOAL] Clear 90 vehicles; at least 15 per direction on every open approach.
[RESTRICTION] Max 4 phases
[RULE] Demand weighting favors North — bias green toward N without dropping S under minimum.
bureauMemo: Corridor waiver is sealed to this ROM. Procurement will deny any ticket that claims someone 'fixed' the N-weight table with a screwdriver.

quickRef.body: North is the heavy lane—bias green that way, but every open approach still has to hit its minimum.

title: Fused E/W Thru
subject: 83-B: Thru Open Circuit
body: East and West through lanes are dead on the schematic; only left and right movements are wired. Route discharge through turns and side spill paths.
bullets: [GOAL] Clear 72 vehicles.
[RESTRICTION] E/W through lanes closed; left and right only on E/W. Max 4 phases
[HAZARD] Turn-heavy flow raises gridlock risk — watch queue depth.
bureauMemo: Utility blames the fuse; Council blames whoever is in the photo. Log turning-phase dwell for packet 17-U and stay out of the frame.

quickRef.body: E/W through is dead—only left and right on those sides. Route discharge through what's still wired.

title: Walk Mandate
subject: 83-C: Crosswalk Phase
body: School dismissal forces an all-red pedestrian phase before traffic can roll again. You still owe full discharge once vehicles resume.
bullets: [GOAL] Clear 80 vehicles; at least 22 per direction.
[RULE] Include EXCLUSIVE_PEDESTRIAN_PHASE.GO in the phase list. Max 6 phases
[RESTRICTION] Long walk phases inflate flow-audit dwell — keep override windows tight.
bureauMemo: School Board staff time the override clock. A fat vehicular green during walk posts straight to their docket — keep the paper slice short.

quickRef.body: You need an all-red walk phase in the phase list—EXCLUSIVE_PEDESTRIAN_PHASE—before cars roll again.

title: PLGA Yield
subject: 84-A: Yield Merge
body: Where geometry allows, use .YIELD on turns so gap logic shares green instead of burning extra banks. Efficiency audit is watching cycle time.
bullets: [GOAL] Clear 95 vehicles.
[RULE] At least two movements must use .YIELD. Max 4 phases
[RESTRICTION] Stay inside thermal duty; do not buy relief with unlimited phases.
bureauMemo: Operator Miller's old EEPROM dumps are exhibit material now. Do not cite them in an incident defense unless you enjoy depositions.

quickRef.body: Don't burn extra phases on every permissive left. Use .YIELD and let turns go when the gap is there.

title: Queue Compare
subject: 84-B: Dynamic Insert
body: Sensor heads are live: use IF (QUEUE…) with phase_insert when depth crosses your threshold. Each comparator line hits the corridor BOM.
bullets: [GOAL] Clear 100 vehicles.
[RULE] At least one IF (QUEUE…) with a matching phase_insert. Max 4 phases; conditionals allowed.
[RESTRICTION] Hardware cost ceiling 1680.
initialCode:
phase(1):
    NORTH_STRAIGHT.GO
    SOUTH_STRAIGHT.GO
    EAST_STRAIGHT.GO
    WEST_STRAIGHT.GO
if (QUEUE.NORTH_LEFT > 5):
    phase_insert(NORTH_LEFT.GO)
bureauMemo: Procurement classifies each comparator arm as Class-B sensor spend. They capped this corridor below the usual multiplex allowance out of spite.

quickRef.body: IF (QUEUE…) watches lane depth; pair it with phase_insert so a backed-up movement gets a green when it crosses your threshold.

title: Split E/W Mast
subject: 84-C: Heavy West
body: Westbound truck envelopes need their own phase; do not hard-green opposing E/W movements in the same macro-phase. Split the mast banks.
bullets: [GOAL] Clear 82 vehicles; at least 22 per direction.
[RESTRICTION] Max 6 phases; conditionals allowed. Sequence W_LEFT/W_STRAIGHT/W_RIGHT separate from the east mast.
[HAZARD] Opposing E/W hard .GO in one phase risks kinetic overlap fault.
bureauMemo: Safety wants split-bank screenshots where west heavy never shares a phase with east green. Auditors treat silence on the annunciator as a personality trait.

title: Eight-Bank MUX
subject: 85-A: 8-Phase Cap
body: All twelve vehicular movements are strapped and demand is pegged high. Run eight banks that keep the conflict matrix legal and queues off the saturation rail.
bullets: [GOAL] Clear 110 vehicles; at least 28 per direction.
[RESTRICTION] Up to 8 phases; conditionals allowed. Hardware cost ceiling 1850.
[HAZARD] Deep queues (gridlock) or green-sum overheat end the run.
bureauMemo: Capital-grade multiplex: one thermal shunt voids the warranty and gives the vendor a holiday bonus. Finance is not joking.

title: EMS South
subject: 85-B: Hospital Lane
body: Southbound is the EMS strap; SOUTH_STRAIGHT must not stack deep. Use comparator-driven phase_insert the moment depth climbs past nominal.
bullets: [GOAL] Clear 95 vehicles.
[RESTRICTION] Max 4 phases; conditionals allowed.
[HAZARD] More than 5 vehicles waiting on SOUTH_STRAIGHT triggers GRIDLOCK_DETECTED.
bureauMemo: Hospital liaison files HL-22 when EMS stacks like a sale lot. They keep a wall of shame by the water cooler; your name is not interior design.

title: Sandbox
subject: SVE: Unscored Rack
body: Validation partition is mounted with full hardware and no procurement cap. Bureau ledger ignores SVE logs — burn images here before a scored filing.
bullets: [MODE] No quota; unscored practice.
[RULE] Tune demand and logic manually.
[RESTRICTION] Scored dockets still need real BOM numbers at upload.
bureauMemo: Procurement cannot see this rack; pretend that makes you brave instead of temporarily unobserved.
